question_text: In a sealed syringe, the volume of gas is reduced from 50 cm³ to 25 cm³ by pushing the plunger at constant temperature. The initial pressure was 100 kPa. What will the new pressure be, assuming the gas behaves ideally?
explanation: Using Boyle's Law (P₁V₁ = P₂V₂) at constant temperature: 100 × 50 = P₂ × 25. Therefore P₂ = 5000/25 = 200 kPa. When volume is halved, pressure doubles because the gas particles collide with the walls more frequently.
